note: FROZEN pre-registration (#976) — never edit; delete deliberately to regenerate.

predictions.claim_natural: During the first 7 days (Days 1–7), nightly time-in-bed will consistently reach the 7.5-hour target window defined by the 9:00 PM bedtime and 4:30 AM wake time, meaning the wearable will log at least 7.5 hours in bed on each of the 7 nights.

Over the first 14 days (Days 1–14), sleep consistency — defined as both the 9:00 PM bedtime and 4:30 AM wake time being hit within a 30-minute window on each logged night — will trend upward as the fixed schedule reinforces circadian anchoring, with more compliant nights in week 2 than in week 1.

Daily calorie log entries will show adherence to the 1500 kcal/day target on at least 5 out of every 7 days during the first 14 days, as the structured 11:30am-7:30pm eating window mechanically constrains intake opportunity.

Logged daily protein intake will meet or exceed the 170 g floor on at least 4 out of the first 7 days, as hitting 170 g within a 1500 kcal budget requires deliberate high-protein food selection that typically takes several days to calibrate from a cold start.

During the first 14 days, daily walking will consistently meet the 3-mile minimum on all 5 post-work weekdays per week, as logged by GPS or step count, because the ramp discipline explicitly schedules walking after work daily and caps intensity to avoid overreach in week 1.

Zone 2 cardio minutes logged per week will be below the 150-minute/week target during the first 2 weeks, because the plan explicitly restricts zone 2 to RPE-guided effort only (no heart-rate-locked sessions) for the first 2 weeks, making it likely that actual accumulated zone 2 time falls short of the 150-minute target while the athlete calibrates perceived exertion.

Adherence to the 11:30am–7:30pm eating window will be maintained on at least 6 out of 7 days during the first 14 days, as the structured ramp discipline (reduced volume, no failure sets in week 1) lowers psychological overwhelm and makes behavioral compliance easier to sustain early.

The 9:00 PM bedtime target will be logged as met on at least 5 out of 7 nights in the first 7 days, as the fixed 4:30 AM wake time creates a concrete external anchor that reinforces consistent sleep-onset behavior from Day 1.

Body weight will trend downward from the 315.65 lb start weight over the first 14 days, driven by the 1500 kcal/day deficit combined with daily ~3-mile walking and 5 gym days/week of Foundation-phase strength training.

Daily protein intake logs will meet or exceed the 170 g protein floor on at least 10 of the first 14 days, as the 1500 kcal/day plan is explicitly structured around that minimum protein target within the 11:30am–7:30pm eating window.

Within the first 14 days, daily post-meal glucose excursions will show a consistent downward trend, driven by the 8-hour eating window (11:30am–7:30pm) combined with the 1500 kcal/day intake and daily post-work walking (~3 miles), resulting in measurably lower peak readings by day 14 compared to day 1.

Within the first 14 days, fasting glucose readings logged each morning (taken before the 11:30am eating window opens, after the overnight fast beginning at 7:30pm — a minimum 16-hour fast) will trend downward across the 14-day window, consistent with the caloric deficit of 1500 kcal/day and the enforced nightly fasting period.

Resting heart rate (RHR) will trend downward over the first 14 days, consistent with the plan's daily walking (~3 miles) and 150 min/week Zone 2 target combined with the 7.5-hour sleep protocol beginning Day 1, moving toward the stated long-term biomarker target of 55 bpm.

HRV will trend upward over the first 14 days, driven by the ramp-discipline rules (reduced volume in Week 1, no failure sets, Zone 2 by RPE only for the first 2 weeks) limiting autonomic stress while the 9:00 PM–4:30 AM sleep window provides consistent 7.5-hour recovery, moving toward the stated biomarker target of 50 ms.

Within the first 14 days, daily calorie log entries will show at least 12 out of 14 days recorded (≥85% adherence rate), establishing a sufficient N=1 data density baseline for statistical tracking of the 1500 kcal/day target.

Across the first 14 days, the within-subject variance in daily step count will be high (coefficient of variation >15%) as the roughly 6,000–7,000 step daily walking target self-calibrates during the ramp discipline week-1 reduced-volume phase, reflecting expected early-experiment noise in a single-subject time series.

hypotheses.hypothesis: On days when logged calories are at or under the 1500 kcal plan target, next-day weight will be lower than after days over target.
hypotheses.evidence: Pre-registered at genesis from the plan itself: 1500 kcal/day target from a 315.65 lb start (config/user_goals.json); MacroFactor logs daily calories and Withings logs daily weight, so both arms are directly measurable.
hypotheses.confirmation_criteria: Mean next-day weight at least 0.1 lbs lower on adherent days within 30 days, 95% CI excluding 0.
hypotheses.actionable_if_confirmed: Hold the 1,500 kcal baseline through the Foundation phase; escalate only if the trend stalls.

hypotheses.hypothesis: On days reaching roughly 6,000+ steps (the 3-mile daily-movement floor), next-day Whoop recovery will be higher than after low-movement days.
hypotheses.evidence: Pre-registered at genesis from the plan itself: daily movement target '3 miles walking minimum (steps will vary by stride length, roughly 6,000-7,000 steps)' (config/user_goals.json); Apple Health logs steps and Whoop logs recovery daily.
hypotheses.confirmation_criteria: Next-day recovery at least 3 points higher on 6,000+ step days within 30 days, 95% CI excluding 0.
hypotheses.actionable_if_confirmed: Protect the daily walk as a recovery lever, not just an energy-expenditure line.
